icon.description: URL to the agent's avatar

tools.description: List of connected tools

actions.description: List of connected actions

post.description:
Creates a new BYO Agent that delegates conversations to an external AI Agent. Use this endpoint to register a BYO (Bring Your Own) agent powered by frameworks like Mastra, CrewAI, or LangGraph.

**Validation:** All referenced tools and actions are validated before the agent is created.

get.description: Retrieves all BYO Agents configured for your CometChat app. Use this endpoint to view all registered BYO (Bring Your Own) agents and their current configuration.

post.description:
Defines custom tools that agents can invoke during conversations.

**Naming:** The tool name must be unique per application.

**Type:** When `doNotExecute` is `true`, the type is set to `tool`. Otherwise, it is set to `action`.

get.description:
Retrieves all tools configured for your CometChat app. Use this endpoint to view available tools and actions, or filter by type.

**Filtering:** Use the `type` query parameter to filter by `action` or `tool`.
Retrieves a specific tool by its unique name. Use this endpoint to inspect a tool's configuration, parameters, and description.

put.description:
Updates an existing tool by its unique name. Use this endpoint to modify a tool's description, parameters, or behavior.

**Restrictions:** The tool name cannot be changed after creation. The provided tool configuration (name, description, parameters) is merged with the existing values.

delete.description: Deletes a tool by its unique name.

get.description:
Retrieves the list of AI models available for agents. Use this endpoint to discover supported models before creating or updating an agent.

**Model details:** Each model includes its provider, configuration settings, and whether it is the default. The default model is used when no model is explicitly specified during agent creation.

200.description: List of variables (flat array or grouped by category)

get.description:
Retrieves all enabled variables (predefined and custom) for the application. Use this endpoint to view variable definitions and understand where they are used.

**Usage tracking:** Each variable includes information showing which agents reference it in their instructions and which API tools use it.

**Grouping:** Set `groupByCategory` to `true` to group results by category (e.g., auth, custom); otherwise results are returned as a flat array.

post.description:
Creates a new agent with optional tools, API tools, MCP servers, and frontend actions. Use this endpoint to set up a fully configured agent in a single request.

**Validation:** All referenced tools, API tools, and MCP servers are validated before creation.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: affix
parameters.description: To paginate over results, use the value of affix as prepend or append.

parameters.name: createdAt
parameters.description: To be used with affix for pagination.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search term to filter agents by name, description, or other fields

get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of agents for your CometChat app. Use this endpoint to browse, search, and manage your agents.

**Search:** Use the `search` parameter to filter agents by name, description, or UID.

Retrieves all custom variables defined for the application. Use this endpoint to view and manage dynamic variables used in agent instructions.

**Variable types:** Custom variables can source values from message metadata, user metadata, or constant values. Each variable includes its source type, resolution path, and default value.

post.description:
Creates a new custom variable for the application. Use this endpoint to define dynamic values that can be injected into agent instructions.

**Source types:** Variables can source values from message metadata, user metadata, or a constant value.

**Naming:** Variable names must be unique within the app and follow the pattern `[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_.]*`.

patch.description:
Updates an existing custom variable by its ID. Use this endpoint to modify a variable's source, default value, or other properties.

**Partial updates:** Only the provided fields are updated; omitted fields remain unchanged.

**Restrictions:** The variable name cannot be changed after creation.

delete.description:
Deletes a custom variable by its ID.

**Impact:** Any agent instructions referencing this variable will no longer resolve its value.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: affix
parameters.description: To paginate over results, use the value of affix as prepend or append.

parameters.name: createdAt
parameters.description: To be used with affix for pagination.

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of API tools for the application. Use this endpoint to browse and manage custom API tool configurations.

post.description:
Creates a new API tool configuration for the application. Use this endpoint to define a custom API endpoint that agents can call during conversations.

**Uniqueness:** The slug must be unique per application.

get.description: Retrieves a specific API tool by its slug. Use this endpoint to inspect an API tool's configuration, parameters, and connected agents.

patch.description: Updates an existing API tool by its slug. Use this endpoint to modify an API tool's parameters, description, or endpoint configuration.

delete.description: Deletes an API tool by its slug.

post.description:
Creates a new MCP (Model Context Protocol) server configuration for the application. Use this endpoint to register an MCP server that agents can connect to.

**Uniqueness:** The slug must be unique per application.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: affix
parameters.description: To paginate over results, use the value of affix as prepend or append.

parameters.name: createdAt
parameters.description: To be used with affix for pagination.

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of MCP servers for the application. Use this endpoint to browse and manage MCP server configurations.
Retrieves a specific MCP server by its slug. Use this endpoint to inspect an MCP server's configuration and connected agents.

patch.description: Updates an existing MCP server by its slug. Use this endpoint to modify an MCP server's configuration or connection details.

delete.description: Deletes an MCP server by its slug.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: affix
parameters.description: To paginate over results, use the value of affix as prepend or append.

parameters.name: createdAt
parameters.description: To be used with affix for pagination.

Triggered when a user clicks a button

data.name: User Click Action
data.description: Triggered when a user clicks a button

tool.name: onUserClick
tool.description: Triggered when a user clicks a button

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of frontend actions for the application. Use this endpoint to browse and manage frontend action configurations.

description.example: Triggered when a user clicks a button
Triggered when a user clicks a button

post.description:
Creates a new frontend action for the application. Use this endpoint to define a client-side UI operation that agents can trigger during conversations.

**Uniqueness:** The trigger function must be unique per application.

description.example: Triggered when a user clicks a button
Triggered when a user clicks a button

get.description: Retrieves a specific frontend action by its unique ID. Use this endpoint to inspect a frontend action's configuration, parameters, and trigger function.

description.example: Triggered when a user clicks a button
Triggered when a user clicks a button

patch.description: Updates an existing frontend action by its unique ID. Use this endpoint to modify a frontend action's parameters, description, or trigger function.

delete.description: Deletes a frontend action by its unique ID.

post.description:
Generates presigned URLs and creates upload records for one or more files. Use this endpoint to prepare files for upload to the knowledge base.

**Workflow:** After receiving the presigned URLs, upload the files directly to the provided URLs, then use the Add Knowledge Base Files endpoint to associate them with an agent.

201.description: Presigned URLs generated and upload records created successfully

s3Key.description: S3 object key for the upload

presignedUrl.description: Presigned URL for direct S3 upload

delete.description:
Deletes an uploaded file and all associated data from the knowledge base by its unique ID.

**Cleanup:** Removes the file, associated embeddings, and any agent references to this file.

message.example: File and all associated data deleted successfully

example.message: File and all associated data deleted successfully

post.description:
Creates a new text-based knowledge base entry. Use this endpoint to add custom text content that agents can reference during conversations.

**Processing:** Accepts a title and text content, converts it to a Markdown file, and adds it to the knowledge base for indexing.

value.title: Company Policy Document
value.text: This document outlines the company policies and procedures for remote work, including communication guidelines, working hours expectations, and performance metrics.

201.description: Text detail created and uploaded to S3 successfully

message.example: Text detail created and uploaded successfully

uniqueId.description: Unique identifier for the text detail

s3Key.description: S3 object key for the uploaded file

example.message: Text detail created and uploaded successfully

get.description: Retrieves all text-based knowledge base entries for the application. Use this endpoint to view and manage text content in the knowledge base.
Retrieves a specific text-based knowledge base entry by its unique ID. Use this endpoint to inspect the content and status of a text entry.

patch.description:
Updates a specific text-based knowledge base entry by its unique ID. Use this endpoint to modify the title or content of an existing text entry.

**Re-indexing:** The content is re-uploaded and the entry status is reset to indexing.

value.title: Updated Company Policy Document
value.text: This is the updated document content with new policies and procedures...

delete.description:
Deletes a text-based knowledge base entry and all associated data by its unique ID.

**Cleanup:** Removes the text content, associated embeddings, and any agent references to this entry.

parameters.name: uniqueId
parameters.description: Unique text entry ID to delete

message.example: Text detail and all associated data deleted successfully

example.message: Text detail and all associated data deleted successfully

get.description: Retrieves knowledge base records for the application. Use this endpoint to browse all knowledge base sources including files, text entries, and websites.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: To be used with affix for pagination.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: affix
parameters.description: To paginate over results, use the value of affix as prepend or append.

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: End timestamp (Unix timestamp in milliseconds) to filter records created before this time

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Start timestamp (Unix timestamp in milliseconds) to filter records created after this time

200.description: Knowledge base records retrieved successfully with pagination metadata

data.description: Array of knowledge base records (excludes _id, __v, paginationKey)

sourceName.description: Name of the source file or text detail

previous.description: Previous page navigation (only present if previous page exists)

next.description: Next page navigation (only present if next page exists)

get.description: Retrieves paginated pages for a specific knowledge base source file. Use this endpoint to inspect the individual pages extracted from a knowledge base source.

parameters.name: uniqueId
parameters.description: Unique file ID to fetch pages for

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: To be used with affix for pagination.

parameters.name: affix
parameters.description: To paginate over results, use the value of affix as prepend or append.

pageContent.content: This is the extracted text content from the first page of the uploaded document. It contains the information that was indexed into the knowledge base for retrieval during agent conversations.

patch.description:
Retries the indexing process for a specific knowledge base source that failed during its initial processing. Provide the source type and its unique ID to re-trigger vectorization.

**Supported source types:** `uploads`, `text`, `website`.

requestBody.description: Array of unique IDs to query

post.description:
Retrieves the indexing status for one or more knowledge base sources by their IDs. Use this endpoint to monitor the progress of file uploads, text entries, or website crawls being indexed.

**Batch support:** Accepts multiple IDs in a single request to efficiently check status across sources.

Scrapes a website to discover and crawl its pages for the knowledge base. Use this endpoint to add website content as a knowledge source for agents.

**Configuration:** Supports options for URL filtering and crawl depth control.

basic.description: Scrape website and fetch sitemap with basic configuration

post.description:
Scrapes a single webpage to extract its content for the knowledge base. Use this endpoint to add a specific page without crawling an entire website.

**Extraction:** Returns the page title, description, and full content.

description.example: Learn how to get started with our platform

content.example:
# Getting Started

Welcome to our platform...

data.title: Getting Started Guide
data.description: Learn how to get started with our platform
data.content:
# Getting Started

Welcome to our platform...

get.description: Retrieves all URLs discovered during a website crawl. Use this endpoint to review which pages were found before processing them.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: To be used with affix for pagination.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: affix
parameters.description: To paginate over results, use the value of affix as prepend or append.

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Start timestamp (Unix timestamp in milliseconds) to filter records created after this time

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: End timestamp (Unix timestamp in milliseconds) to filter records created before this time

parameters.name: parentId
parameters.description: Unique ID of the parent crawl (from WebPages collection)

200.description: Discovered URLs retrieved successfully with pagination metadata

urlId.description: Unique identifier for the discovered URL

title.description: Page title extracted from the URL

lastCrawledAt.description: Timestamp when URL was last crawled

limit.description: Records per page limit used for this request

count.description: Number of records returned in this page

previous.description: Navigation info for previous page (newer records). Present only if there are newer records available.

cursor.description: Cursor value to use in next request for previous page

next.description: Navigation info for next page (older records). Present only if there are older records available.

cursor.description: Cursor value to use in next request for next page

post.description: Marks discovered URLs for exclusion and initiates batch crawling of the remaining URLs. Use this endpoint to selectively process pages discovered during a website crawl.

requestBody.description: Discovered URLs processing configuration. App ID is taken from header, not body.

post.description:
Retrieves the processing status of discovered URLs by their unique IDs. Use this endpoint to monitor the progress of URL crawling within a parent website crawl.

**Behavior:** Only returns records for existing unique IDs; non-existing IDs are omitted from the response.

requestBody.description: Array of discovered URL unique IDs to query status for

200.description: Status for requested discovered URLs (omits non-existing uniqueIds)

patch.description: Retries the indexing process for specific pages under a website knowledge base source. Pass the unique IDs of the pages to re-index, or send an empty array to automatically retry all pages that failed during a previous indexing attempt.

requestBody.description: Array of discovered URL unique IDs to sync. Only non-excluded URLs will be processed.

sync_specific_urls.description: Process only the specified discovered URLs that are not excluded

delete.description:
Deletes a website crawl and all associated data from the knowledge base by its unique ID.

**Cleanup:** Removes all crawled pages, associated embeddings, and any agent references to this website.

parameters.name: uniqueId
parameters.description: Unique website crawl ID to delete

200.description: Website and all associated data deleted successfully

message.example: Website and all associated data deleted successfully

example.message: File and all associated data deleted successfully

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Search term to filter by name/description

200.description: Categorized enabled items for the agent

tools.name: Gmail
tools.description: Gmail integration

actions.name: Send Email
actions.description: Send an email via Gmail

frontendActions.name: User Click Action
frontendActions.description: Triggered when a user clicks a button

get.description:
Retrieves all enabled items for a specific agent, categorized by type. Use this endpoint to see which tools, frontend actions, API tools, and MCP servers are currently enabled for an agent.

**Categories:** Results are grouped into tools, frontend actions, API tools, and MCP servers. Use query parameters to include or exclude specific categories.

**Search:** Use the `key` parameter to filter enabled items by name or description.

200.description: Tools successfully added to the agent.

patch.description:
Adds one or more tools to a specific agent. Use this endpoint to extend an agent's capabilities with additional tool integrations.

**Request body:** The `tools` array contains tool slugs. Returns the updated agent object.

200.description: Tools successfully removed from the agent.

patch.description:
Removes one or more tools from a specific agent. Use this endpoint to revoke tool access from an agent.

**Request body:** The `tools` array contains tool slugs to remove. Returns the updated agent object.

200.description: MCP servers successfully added to the agent.

patch.description:
Adds one or more MCP servers to a specific agent. Use this endpoint to connect an agent to additional Model Context Protocol servers.

**Request body:** The `mcps` array contains MCP server slugs. Returns the updated agent object.

200.description: MCP servers successfully removed from the agent.

patch.description:
Removes one or more MCP servers from a specific agent. Use this endpoint to disconnect MCP servers from an agent.

**Request body:** The `mcps` array contains MCP server slugs to remove. Returns the updated agent object.

200.description: API tools successfully added to the agent.

patch.description:
Adds one or more API tools to a specific agent. Use this endpoint to give an agent access to custom API tool integrations.

**Request body:** The `apiTools` array contains API tool slugs. Returns the updated agent object.

200.description: API tools successfully removed from the agent.

patch.description:
Removes one or more API tools from a specific agent. Use this endpoint to revoke API tool access from an agent.

**Request body:** The `apiTools` array contains API tool slugs to remove. Returns the updated agent object.

200.description: Frontend actions successfully added to the agent.

patch.description:
Adds one or more frontend actions to a specific agent. Use this endpoint to enable an agent to trigger client-side UI operations.

**Frontend actions** allow agents to interact with the user interface, such as opening dialogs, navigating pages, or updating UI state.

**Request body:** The `frontendActions` array contains frontend action identifiers. Returns the updated agent object.

200.description: Frontend actions successfully removed from the agent.

patch.description:
Removes one or more frontend actions from a specific agent. Use this endpoint to revoke frontend action capabilities from an agent.

**Request body:** The `frontendActions` array contains frontend action identifiers to remove. Returns the updated agent object.
Adds actions to a specific tool attached to an agent.
Removes actions from a specific tool attached to an agent.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Get actions available within a tool (e.g., send-email, draft-email)

get.summary: Get actions available within a tool for agent
get.description: Lists all actions available for a specific tool attached to an agent.
tags: Agent Builder Ready to Use Tools

patch.description:
Updates an existing BYO Agent by its UID. Use this endpoint to modify agent configuration, update referenced tools, or change the connected AI framework.

**Validation:** Referenced tools and actions are validated if provided.

example.message: Agent with UID '863f3501-af1b-46c1-9740-5c725b85daba' has been deleted successfully

delete.description: Deletes a BYO Agent by its UID.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Search term to filter actions by name, description, or slug

200.description: Aggregated search results from all tool sources

get.description:
Searches across all tool types available for an agent, including ready-to-use tools, API tools, and MCP servers. Use this endpoint to find specific tools across all categories.

**Filtering:** Use the `key` parameter to filter by name, description, or slug, and the `slug` parameter to filter by a specific integration.
Retrieves a specific agent by its UID. Use this endpoint to inspect an agent's full configuration, including its tools, model, and instructions.

patch.description:
Updates an existing agent by its UID. Use this endpoint to modify an agent's configuration, instructions, model, or connected tools.

**Validation:** Referenced tools, API tools, and MCP servers are validated if provided.

delete.description: Deletes an agent by its UID.

200.description: Files added to knowledge base successfully

patch.summary: Add Files to Agent Knowledge Base
patch.description:
Associates uploaded files or text entries with an agent's knowledge base. Use this endpoint after uploading files via the Initiate File Uploads endpoint to make them available to the agent.

**Source types:** Specify the source type (e.g., uploads, text) in the path.

200.description: Files removed from knowledge base successfully

patch.summary: Remove Files from Agent Knowledge Base
patch.description:
Removes files or text entries from an agent's knowledge base. Use this endpoint to disassociate specific sources from an agent without deleting the underlying content.

**Source types:** Specify the source type (e.g., uploads, text) in the path.

displayName.description: Display name shown in the UI for this tool

description.description: Description of what the tool does. Required when doNotExecute is false.
description.example: Fetches current weather data for a given location

executionText.description: Text displayed while the tool is executing

name.description: Unique function name for the tool. Must start with a letter, underscore, or dollar sign, followed by alphanumeric characters, underscores, dollar signs, or hyphens.

doNotExecute.description: When true, the tool will not be executed locally and parameters/description become optional

parameters.description: Tool parameter definitions. Required when doNotExecute is false, but can be an empty object. Must be empty or omitted when doNotExecute is true.

icon.description: URL to the icon/avatar of the agent

tools.description: Array of tool names available to this agent

apiTools.description: Array of API tool names available to this agent

mcpServers.description: Array of MCP server names available to this agent

model.description: AI model to use for this agent

modelMetaData.description: Configuration metadata for the AI model

temperature.description: Controls randomness in responses. Lower values make output more focused and deterministic.

maxTokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate in the response.

temperature.description: Controls randomness in responses. Lower values make output more focused and deterministic.

maxTokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate in the response.

tools.description: Array of tool names to add or remove

mcps.description: Array of MCP server slugs to add or remove

apiTools.description: Array of API tool slugs to add or remove

frontendActions.description: Array of frontend action function names to add or remove

uniqueIds.description: Array of file IDs to be added to the knowledge base

actions.description: Array of action names to add or remove

name.description: Unique variable name. Must start with a letter and contain only alphanumeric characters, underscores, and dots.

description.example: The preferred language of the user

sourceType.description: Source from which the variable value is resolved

sourcePath.description: Dot-notation path to extract the value from the source metadata

constantValue.description: Fixed value used when sourceType is constant

defaultValue.description: Fallback value used when the source path does not resolve

description.example: The preferred language of the user

sourceType.description: Source from which the variable value is resolved

sourcePath.description: Dot-notation path to extract the value from the source metadata

constantValue.description: Fixed value used when sourceType is constant

defaultValue.description: Fallback value used when the source path does not resolve

receiverUid.description: Receiver user ID (defaults to agent ID)

sampleQueryParams.description: Sample query parameters to test resolution

description.example: When to use, preconditions, what it returns

basicAuthUsername.description: Basic auth username (required if enableBasicAuth is true)

basicAuthPassword.description: Basic auth password (required if enableBasicAuth is true)

headers.description: HTTP headers as a JSON string (e.g., '{"Content-Type": "application/json", "Authorization": "Bearer token"}')

bodyTemplate.description: Body template for the API request

parameters.description: Array of parameters for the API tool

icon.description: Icon for the API Tool

icon.description: Icon for the API Tool

name.description: Name of the MCP server (human readable)

slug.description: Slug of the MCP server (unique, URL-safe)

icon.description: Icon for the MCP server

name.description: Updated name of the MCP server (human readable)

description.description: Updated description of the MCP server

description.example: The ID of the product to display

name.description: Display name of the frontend action

description.description: Description of what the frontend action does
description.example: Opens a modal displaying product details for the specified product ID

triggerFunction.description: The function name that the frontend will execute (used as unique identifier)

parameters.description: Array of parameters for the frontend action

icon.description: Icon URL for the frontend action

description.description: Description of what the frontend action does
description.example: Opens a modal displaying product details

parameters.description: Array of parameters for the frontend action

icon.description: Icon URL for the frontend action

fileSize.description: Size of the file in bytes

text.example: This document outlines the company policies and procedures...
This is the updated document content with new policies and procedures...

uniqueId.description: Array of unique IDs to query

maxDepth.description: Maximum depth to crawl from the starting URL

maxPages.description: Maximum number of pages to crawl

include.description: URL patterns to include in crawling (substring matching)

exclude.description: URL patterns to exclude from crawling (substring matching)

fetchSitemap.description: Fetch and return sitemap URLs from the website

url.description: Target page URL to crawl and extract content from

exclude.description: Array of unique IDs to exclude from crawling

uniqueId.description: Array of discovered URL unique IDs to query status for

uniqueIds.description: Array of unique IDs to sync for batch processing

type.description: Field type (text, url, boolean, dropdown, toggles)

icon.description: Optional tip icon

success.description: Indicates whether the operation was successful
Indicates whether the operation was successful

type.description: The type of the parameters object

properties.description: Map of parameter property names to their definitions (type and description)

required.description: List of required parameter property names

deleted.description: Indicates whether the resource was deleted
